description:
You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who is enforcing “policy-as-code” across a very small set of YAML policy definition files.

    Current project layout:
      /home/user/policies/policy-001.yaml
      /home/user/policies/policy-002.yaml
      /home/user/policies/policy-003.yaml

    Goal:
      1. Identify every policy file whose enabled flag is not explicitly set to the literal value “true”.
      2. Produce a simple audit log that lists *only* the IDs of the non-compliant policies, one per line, sorted alphabetically.
      3. Save that log to the file /home/user/audit/disabled_policies.log.  
         • If the directory /home/user/audit/ does not already exist you must create it.  
         • The file must end with a single trailing newline; no blank lines elsewhere.

    VERY IMPORTANT – log-file format the grader will check:
      • Path      : /home/user/audit/disabled_policies.log  
      • Encoding  : UTF-8 text  
      • Lines     : `<POLICY_ID>\n` repeated for each non-compliant policy.  
      • Ordering  : Alphabetical (standard ASCII/UTF-8 byte order).  
      • Example   :  
            policy-002\n  
            policy-003\n

    Allowed tooling:
      Use *one* Linux command of your choice (you may chain sub-commands with pipes, &&, || etc.; all of that still counts as a single command line).  
      You must not manually open the files in an editor—everything should be solved through command-line processing.

    When you are done, the grader will:
      1. Read /home/user/audit/disabled_policies.log
      2. Verify that the content matches the exact specification above.

    Hints (do not reveal command):
      • grep/awk/sed/cut/basename/find/sort are all available.  
      • Remember that a file is non-compliant if the enabled field is either missing OR present but NOT set to “true”.

    Deliverable: the correctly-formatted /home/user/audit/disabled_policies.log file created via a single terminal command.
truth:
Initial files shipped with the container:

    1. /home/user/policies/policy-001.yaml  
       -----8<-----  
       id: policy-001  
       name: ensure-ssh-protocol-2  
       enabled: true  
       -----8<-----

    2. /home/user/policies/policy-002.yaml  
       -----8<-----  
       id: policy-002  
       name: disallow-root-login  
       enabled: false  
       -----8<-----

    3. /home/user/policies/policy-003.yaml  
       -----8<-----  
       id: policy-003  
       name: enforce-password-complexity  
       # NOTE: intentionally missing “enabled” key  
       -----8<-----

    Expected artefacts after correct task completion:

    A. Directory /home/user/audit/ must exist (permissions 755 default are acceptable).  
    B. File /home/user/audit/disabled_policies.log must exist and contain exactly:

       policy-002
       policy-003

       (with a single trailing newline character after “policy-003” and no additional blank lines).

    The grader will parse the file, strip no whitespace, and expect byte-for-byte equality to:

       "policy-002\npolicy-003\n"
name: task_000000_350329b8
